summary: Neillia incisa, commonly called lace shrub, is a species of plant in the rose family ( Rosaceae ). It is native to eastern Asia, where it is found in China, Japan, Korea, and Taiwan. In the United States it is commonly cultivated by nurseries as an ornamental, and it has been naturalized in the U.S. state of Virginia. It is expected to become invasive in temperate forests of North America in the future.
